race.name: Skadi Loppet
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers and 1,077 meters of climbing through the Bavarian Forest — named for the Norse goddess of winter and built to break you.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with warm drinks and Bavarian refreshments

climate.description: The Bavarian Forest receives reliable snowfall due to its position catching moisture from both Atlantic and continental weather systems. The Bodenmais area at 900m+ altitude maintains consistent winter conditions. January temperatures on race day typically range from -10°C to 0°C.
challenges: 1,077m of total climbing demands sustained power output
Steep descents through forest require confident technique
Variable conditions — forest shelter versus exposed ridgeline
Central European weather can bring rain-snow transitions

course.primary: Loop course through the Bavarian Forest with a massive 1,077 meters of total ascent — making it one of the most demanding marathon ski races in central Europe. The course climbs through dense spruce and beech forest to exposed ridgelines before plunging back down through tight, technical descents. The terrain is relentless.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through dense mountain forest and ridgeline terrain
course.grooming: Good German-standard grooming, adapted to demanding terrain
features: 1,077m total ascent — among the most demanding in European marathon skiing
Named after Skadi, Norse goddess of winter and mountains
Nearly 100% snow reliability due to altitude and microclimate
Dense Bavarian Forest with spruce and beech canopy
Technical descents through tight forest trails

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: One of the most physically demanding marathon ski races in Europe — 1,077m of climbing in 42km is brutal. Technical descents and relentless terrain reward strong all-round skiers. The Bavarian Forest setting provides reliable snow and atmospheric racing.

history.summary: Founded in 2000, the Skadi Loppet takes its name from the Norse goddess Skadi, associated with winter, mountains, and skiing. The race was created to showcase the Bavarian Forest's exceptional Nordic skiing terrain and has earned a reputation as one of the most physically demanding marathon ski races in central Europe. Its 1,077m of climbing in just 42km is nearly unmatched.
notable_facts: Named after Skadi, the Norse goddess of winter, mountains, and skiing
1,077m of total ascent makes it one of Europe's toughest marathon ski races
The Bavarian Forest is Germany's first national park (established 1970)
Nearly 100% snow reliability — the Bodenmais area is one of Germany's snowiest
Euroloppet member connecting it to the European marathon skiing circuit
The Bavarian Forest borders Sumava National Park in the Czech Republic

videos.title: Skadi Loppet Teaser 2020
videos.channel: Euroloppet Your World of Ski Marathon
videos.description: Skadi Loppet is a Ski Marathon Race of Euroloppet, the most famous Ski Marathon Series in Europe. Skadi Loppet exists since 1990 and celebrates its 30th anniversary in 2020. The race is scheduled on the thrid weekend of March in Bodenmais/ Germany have only been canceled once in 30 years.

videos.title: Skadi Loppet - Langlaufevent am Bretterschachten in Bodenmais
videos.description: Während man sich allerorts schon für den Frühling herausputzt, ist das Bodenmaiser Ski-Zentrum am Wochenende für den letzten großen Wintersport-Event getrimmt worden: Loipen ziehen, Piste präparieren, Start- und Ziel-Raum mit Neuschnee auffüllen, Ski wachsen etc. All das war nötig, um beim Skadi Loppet optimale Bedingungen für die Athleten zu schaffen.
videos.curation_reason: Race coverage showing course preparation and event logistics at the Bretterschachten venue.

videos.title: Reportage Skadi Loppet 2016
videos.description: Das war der Skadi Loppet 2016 mit einem kleinen Blick hinter die Kulissen der Mammut-Veranstaltung in Bodenmais im Bayerischen Wald.
videos.curation_reason: Official race reportage with behind-the-scenes coverage of the event organization and atmosphere.

videos.title: Langlauf Bretterschachten
videos.description:
A tour of the Bretterschachten Loipen on 17 January 2020.   Snow conditions were far from perfect.  The weather changed significantly during lunch (at1:23 in this footage) when the winter sun gave way to clouds and a chilly breeze.

We were accompanied by Guy Beaumont from GB Nordique who had been helping us to improve our techniques throughout the week.  https://www.gbnordique.fr/

Music is 'Tenderness' from www.bensound.com

#NoOrdinaryJob #RedBoots
